rationale: In the literature review, the cited paper is singled out as a prior dynamic study of synergy between television and magazine advertising with budget-allocation implications. The citing paper then explicitly positions its own contribution as studying a different synergy source, namely planned celebrity advertising and unplanned performance-driven exposure, and deriving allocation implications in that context. This is more than a background list citation because it helps define the paper's conceptual positioning and claimed contribution, even without direct method reuse.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Naik and Raman (2003) as a key prior synergy reference and extends that line to celebrity endorsement by analyzing synergy between planned ads and unplanned exposure. It carries the synergy-and-allocation perspective into a new setting with product life-cycle coordination results.
